name: KeyTrain's Key Training — Identity & Access Security

questions.text: A help desk ticket reports repeated lockouts for one user after they traveled. Logs show failed logins from two countries within minutes. What is the BEST first response?

options.text: Ignore lockouts if the user confirms they remember their password
Disable the account permanently without investigation
Share the user's password hash with the manager to verify identity
Treat as potential credential compromise: force password reset, revoke sessions, and verify MFA enrollment

questions.explanation: Impossible-travel patterns suggest stolen credentials; reset and session revocation limit abuse while MFA reduces replay risk.

questions.text: Your org is rolling out passwordless sign-in for contractors. Which control MOST directly reduces phishing of static passwords?

options.text: Shared contractor kiosk accounts
Annual security awareness posters
Phishing-resistant authenticators (FIDO2 / passkeys) bound to the identity provider
Longer password complexity rules only

questions.explanation: Passwordless FIDO2 resists credential phishing; complexity alone does not stop real-time proxy attacks.

questions.text: SSO logs show an application accepting SAML assertions after certificate rollover, but only for one legacy app. What should you verify FIRST?

options.text: Whether users cleared browser cache
DNS TTL for the marketing site
If the app supports biometric unlock on mobile
The service provider metadata and signing certificate trust store match the new IdP certificate

questions.explanation: SAML trust breaks when SP metadata is stale; certificate mismatch is the typical root cause after IdP rotation.

questions.text: An executive refuses hardware tokens but approves SMS codes for banking-style apps. Which risk should you document in the exception?

options.text: SMS messages are always encrypted end-to-end
Hardware tokens cannot be used on laptops
SIM-swap and SS7 attacks can bypass SMS OTP without attacking the application directly
MFA fatigue only affects push notifications

questions.explanation: SMS OTP is better than nothing but is not phishing-resistant; SIM swap is a known bypass path.

questions.text: Users report MFA push approval prompts they did not initiate. What is the BEST immediate mitigation?

options.text: Disable MFA globally for 24 hours
Increase session timeout to 30 days
Allow users to approve pushes from unknown locations
Enforce number matching or phishing-resistant MFA and investigate for password spray preceding pushes

questions.explanation: Unsolicited pushes indicate MFA fatigue attacks; number matching and investigating spray reduce success rates.

questions.text: During a tabletop, the team must choose MFA for privileged break-glass accounts. What is the MOST appropriate design?

options.text: Same SMS OTP as standard users for simplicity
No MFA on break-glass to speed recovery
Shared TOTP seed in a wiki page
Hardware tokens stored in a physical safe with dual control and separate from daily user MFA

questions.explanation: Break-glass accounts need strongest factors and procedural controls; shared seeds defeat MFA purpose.

questions.text: A junior analyst discovers they can add themselves to a cloud admin group via a misconfigured automation role. What is the BEST remediation sequence?

options.text: Delete all automation in the environment
Promote the analyst to admin so they can monitor themselves
Remove excessive permissions from the role, audit CloudTrail for prior abuse, and enforce least privilege on CI pipelines
Publish the role ARN in the team chat for transparency

questions.explanation: Fix the permission path, then determine if the flaw was exploited; CI roles are a common escalation vector.

questions.text: Endpoint EDR flags PowerShell downloading a script that modifies local admin group membership. Which indicator BEST supports privilege escalation?

options.text: User opened a PDF brochure
Antivirus definition update
Use of a living-off-the-land binary to run unsigned code that adds a new local administrator
Scheduled Windows Update reboot

questions.explanation: Adding local admins via LOLBins is classic escalation after initial access.

questions.text: A service account in Active Directory has unconstrained delegation. Why is this a critical escalation risk?

options.text: Compromise of the service account can yield Kerberos TGTs usable against other hosts
It disables NTLM company-wide
It only affects password expiration policy
It encrypts all file shares automatically

questions.explanation: Unconstrained delegation allows TGT theft and lateral movement—high severity misconfiguration.

questions.text: HR terminates an employee, but their SaaS account stays active two weeks because provisioning is manual. Which control gap does this illustrate?

options.text: Missing patch Tuesday calendar
Overly strict DLP on USB drives
Joiner-mover-leaver process failure and lack of automated deprovisioning tied to HRIS
Insufficient MFA on printers

questions.explanation: Stale accounts are a top misuse vector; HR-driven deprovisioning closes the window.

questions.text: Shared credentials for a production monitoring dashboard are posted in a team channel. What is the BEST replacement?

options.text: Add the password to the email signature for visibility
Rotate the shared password monthly without changing practice
Disable logging to reduce evidence
Individual accounts with RBAC, optional break-glass procedure, and secrets removed from chat history

questions.explanation: Shared accounts prevent attribution; RBAC and individual identity restore accountability.

questions.text: Audit finds contractors using former employees' accounts. What policy enforcement MOST directly stops this?

options.text: Increasing CCTV retention
Blocking USB ports on laptops
Longer minimum password length only
Periodic access reviews and automated disablement when employment status changes

questions.explanation: Access reviews and lifecycle automation address account sharing and ex-employee reuse.

questions.text: Dark web monitoring shows corporate emails in a breach dump. What is the FIRST technical action aligned to credential abuse defense?

options.text: Publish the dump internally for awareness
Block all outbound email for a week
Force password reset and invalidate refresh tokens for affected identities
Disable TLS on the mail gateway

questions.explanation: Reset and token revocation contain reuse of stolen passwords and session artifacts.

questions.text: Authentication logs show 40,000 failed logins against one legacy IMAP service from one ASN. Which attack type is MOST likely?

options.text: Physical tailgating at the front desk
SAST false positive on a web app
ARP spoofing on the data center VLAN
Password spraying against a single protocol before attempting valid pairs elsewhere

questions.explanation: High-volume failures on one service suggest spray, often preceding targeted login attempts.

questions.text: Developers store API keys in public GitHub repos. Which practice BEST reduces credential abuse long term?

options.text: Secret scanning in CI/CD, pre-commit hooks, and short-lived scoped tokens
Rename repos to private after launch only
Disable Git entirely
Use one global API key for all microservices

questions.explanation: Prevention and rotation limit blast radius when keys leak; scanning catches commits early.
